steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage opens with "I have less need now than when I was young" — a past/present comparison. The underlined portion must logically follow as examples of what the speaker needed in youth but no longer needs now.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: reframes the underlined content as a general observation about people, ignoring the speaker's personal past/present contrast. ✗
  • Choice B — 'examples of youthful needs the speaker has outgrown': correctly identifies the two behaviors — sharing herself with every comer, shaping thoughts into words — as what she needed when young but no longer needs, directly fulfilling the comparison the opening sets up. ✓
  • Choice C: treats the content as a recalled episode rather than illustrations of a continuing contrast. ✗
  • Choice D: misreads the behaviors as current habits rather than past ones the speaker has moved beyond. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
